title: Docs: Add troubleshooting for react-docgen-typescript in monorepos
body:
Closes #

<!-- If your PR is related to an issue, provide the number(s) above; if it resolves multiple issues, be sure to break them up (e.g. "closes #1000, closes #1001"). -->

<!--

Thank you for contributing to Storybook! Please submit all PRs to the `next` branch unless they are specific to the current release. Storybook maintainers cherry-pick bug and documentation fixes into the `main` branch as part of the release process, so you shouldn't need to worry about this. For additional guidance: https://storybook.js.org/docs/contribute

-->

## What I did

Adds a troubleshooting entry to the TypeScript configuration docs explaining why inherited props are missing when using `react-docgen-typescript` with workspace packages in a monorepo.

**Root cause:** The `@joshwooding/vite-plugin-react-docgen-typescript` Vite plugin creates a TS program from files matching its `include` glob (default: `**/**.tsx`), resolved from the Storybook project directory. Workspace package files are outside that directory and aren't included in the program, so inherited types (e.g. MUI `ButtonProps`) can't be resolved.

**Fix:** Add workspace source files to the `include` option in `reactDocgenTypescriptOptions`.

Addresses https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/discussions/33837  
Reproduction: https://github.com/kasperpeulen/storybook-rdt-workspace-repro

author: kasperpeulen

comments.author: kylegach
kylegach
kylegach
kylegach
comments.body:
```suggestion
  ### Inherited args are missing for components from workspace packages
```

comments.author: kylegach
comments.body:
```suggestion
  If you're using `react-docgen-typescript` in a monorepo with npm/yarn/pnpm workspaces, you may find that components imported from a workspace package are missing inherited args (e.g., MUI's `ButtonProps`), while the same component imported locally works fine.
```

comments.author: kylegach
comments.body:
```suggestion
  Adjust the path (`../../packages/ui/src/**/*.tsx`) to match your monorepo layout. Note that `tsconfigPath` only affects compiler options. It does not change which files are included in the TypeScript program.
```

Can you clarify the `tsconfigPath` part? It seems to be missing some context.

comments.author: kasperpeulen
comments.body: Done — added context explaining why someone might expect `tsconfigPath` to help here. ✅
